latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 15:57
RNS Number : 3057G
Synthomer PLC
29 May 2026
29 May 2026
Synthomer plc
(the "Company")
Total voting rights
The following information is disclosed in accordance with DTR 5.6.1R of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority (the "
FCA
").
As at the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital consists of 163,997,629 ordinary shares of £0.01 each (the "
Ordinary Shares
"), each carrying the right to one vote at a general meeting of the Company. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 163,997,629.
The figure in the preceding sentence may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Ordinary Shares under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries:
Faisal Tabbah, Vice President Investor Relations
Tel: +44 (0) 7813 210 809

latest.title: Notice of AGM
latest.content:
Today 17:21
RNS Number : 5340F
Synthomer PLC
22 May 2026
Synthomer plc
Notice of Annual General Meeting 2026
Synthomer plc (the "Company") announces that it has today published the notice of its 2026 annual general meeting ("AGM") on its website at
www.synthomer.com
.
The Company's AGM will be held at 10.00 am on 22 June 2026.
In accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.1, copies of the following documents are being submitted to the Financial Conduct Authority and will shortly be available for inspection via the National Storage Mechanism at:
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
:
·
Notice of 2026 annual general meeting; and
·
Form of proxy.
Copies of the notice of AGM and the 2025 Annual Report and Accounts are also available on the Company's website or on request from the Company Secretary, Synthomer plc, 10 Greycoat Place, London SW1P 1SB.
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI): 213800EHT3TI1KPQQJ56.

latest.title: Application for Admission to Trading
latest.content:
Today 13:51
RNS Number : 4730D
Synthomer PLC
07 May 2026
Synthomer plc(the "Company")
Application for Admission of Further Securities to Trading
Further to the Company's announcement on 30 April 2026 of its final results for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the refinancing of its key debt facilities, the Company has allotted and issued 430,008 new ordinary shares of £0.01 each in the capital of the Company (representing approximately 0.26% of the Company's enlarged share capital following such issuance) (the "
New Ordinary Shares
") to certain of the Group's lenders in satisfaction of certain of the fees payable to them in connection with the refinancing.
An application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
LSE
") for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on the LSE's main market for listed securities ("
Admission
"). It is expected that Admission will become effective on or before 8.00 a.m. on 8 May 2026.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: SYNT.L builds and sells synthetic financial instruments that mimic the performance of real assets, allowing investors to gain exposure without holding the underlying securities.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Synthetic products give traders and institutions a cheaper, more flexible way to hedge or speculate on markets that might otherwise be hard to reach. SYNT.L’s tools help spread risk across a wider range of assets, which keeps the market more resilient.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in a restructuring phase. Recent filings show a new chief financial officer and a shift in share ownership, indicating management is trying to shore up finances. The stock trades at a very low price relative to its past highs, suggesting a heavy discount.

rns_narrative: The company has just wrapped up an annual meeting, updated its voting structure, and announced a change to its CFO. These moves point to a management overhaul aimed at stabilising finances.

Financial reports for the latest quarter are out, showing continued cash burn but also a modest improvement in revenue streams. The company is also working on a new product line that could broaden its customer base.
A refinancing update was released, hinting that SYNT.L is looking for fresh capital to keep operations running and to fund the upcoming product launch.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are excited about the new CFO and the possibility of a product rollout that could bring in fresh revenue.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company is still burning cash and may need more debt or a sale of assets to stay afloat.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is low but slowly warming as corporate changes begin to surface.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat, meaning no external hype is building. That’s a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings release could show a better-than‑expected profit margin if the new product line starts generating revenue.
A partnership announcement with a larger financial institution could give the firm credibility and attract institutional investors.
A sudden spike in trading volume at the 50% support level, coupled with a breakout above the short‑term moving average, would signal a potential bounce.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor for a 20‑30% jump in volume, a positive earnings surprise, or any news about new debt or equity financing. A clear change in the capital structure would be a strong confirmation of a rebound.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price looks like it’s still in the early stages of a deep crash. A mean‑reversion is likely once the market recognises the firm’s intrinsic value, which could happen in the next 3‑6 months if the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has likely fallen 70‑90% from its all‑time high. Even a 50% recovery to halfway would double the current price, giving a 100% upside. The CFO change and potential new product launch add value, while the tight price range suggests a quick bounce could occur.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is still burning cash and has no clear revenue growth yet. If it fails to secure financing or the new product stalls, the price could slide further, wiping out the current investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could gain 2‑3 times the amount you risk. For example, risking 10% of your capital could yield a 30‑40% return if the price rebounds to 50% of its peak.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes at the 50% support level. Size the position so that a 10% drop would hit your stop, and aim for a 20‑30% profit target in stages.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear earnings beat or partnership announcement. Look for a 20% rise in volume and a break above the 10‑day moving average as the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or is delisted, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SYNT.L is a deeply discounted synthetic‑securities firm that has just begun a management overhaul; if it secures financing or launches a new product, the upside is high, but continued cash burn remains a real risk.
